review_model.description: BSI employs a modular review system where different expert panels evaluate distinct dimensions of the technical documentation in parallel. Each dimension issues its own findings document.

dimensions.focus: Device description, intended purpose, GSPR checklist compliance
dimensions.characteristics: Focuses on foundational device identity and regulatory compliance baseline

dimensions.focus: Clinical evaluation, equivalence demonstration, clinical data quality
dimensions.characteristics: Deep dive into clinical evidence sufficiency and MDCG 2020-5 compliance

dimensions.focus: Device design, software, cybersecurity, risk management
dimensions.characteristics: Cross-functional technical scrutiny; often issues the most detailed questions
Specialized review for devices with sterilization/microbial control requirements

dimensions.focus: Progressive depth review across multiple submission rounds
dimensions.characteristics: Each feedback builds on previous findings; issues compound if not fully addressed
Full-scope audit covering TDAR, CEAR, and SVR dimensions simultaneously

by_cer_section.note: Feedback/DR类跨模块文档，需引入二级子分类

severity_profile.interpretation: BSI issues almost exclusively Major findings. Minor items are rare (only 1/50). This indicates BSI operates a high-threshold review model where only substantive issues are escalated to formal findings.

source_type_distribution.characteristics: Structured question list; often the first-round interaction
Formal deficiency document with mandatory response format
DR with inline annotations or response tracking

persistent_defect_pattern.root_cause: 企业对NB核心关切理解偏差——'软组织凝固'预期用途缺乏临床证据支撑
GSPR清单非适用性理由的系统性编制能力不足

round_behavior.interpretation: BSI multi-round persistence indicates fundamental misunderstanding of regulatory requirements rather than simple documentation gaps. When the same defect type appears across 3+ rounds, it signals a cognitive gap at the manufacturer level.

regulatory_reference_preference.description: MDR Article 10 (General obligations of manufacturers) — most cited
ISO 13485 / ISO 14971 — quality management and risk management
MDCG guidance documents — secondary clinical guidance
MEDDEV 2.7/1 rev.4 — legacy clinical evaluation guidance

language_patterns: Formal regulatory language with precise article references
Numbered question lists with mandatory response format
NCR documents bilingual (Turkish/English) with structured tables
extraction_risks: NCR bilingual format may confuse language detection
Feedback documents span multiple technical domains — single CER section mapping insufficient
Review Questions often reference external document IDs that must be cross-checked

device_category_sensitivity.note: 预期用途描述需要详细临床证据支撑
GSPR清单非适用性理由是核心关注点
有源设备需同时满足软件、数据一致性、辐射安全
高风险器械的设备描述完整性是准入第一道门槛
多维度并行审评——机械/软件/灭菌/临床四重标准

deerflow_pre_check_rules.description: When submitting to BSI, apply these pre-submission checks BEFORE Writer Agent output

checks.action: Verify every GSPR item has either: (a) full compliance evidence, or (b) justified non-applicability with detailed rationale
checks.rationale: BSI flags GS-001 in 18% of findings; GSPR checklist is their #2 overall concern

checks.action: Confirm §2 contains: device name, model/variant list, EMDN code, MDR Class+Rule, intended purpose with patient population, contraindications, AND novel feature description
checks.rationale: DO-001 is BSI's #1 defect at 34%; Technical Review Questions frequently challenge device identity completeness

checks.action: Verify equivalence table follows MDCG 2020-5 three-pillar structure; clinical/technical/biological evidence each has standalone justification
checks.rationale: EQ-001 concentrated in Review Questions; BSI Clinical Review dimension explicitly checks MDCG 2020-5 compliance

checks.action: Verify software lifecycle documentation list includes all referenced files; traceability from test reports to design records is demonstrable
checks.rationale: SW-001 and DT-001 cluster in Feedback rounds for complex active devices like SPECT-CT

checks.action: If same defect type appeared in previous round, require explicit root-cause analysis and systemic fix evidence (not just document update)
checks.rationale: Persistent defects across R1→R3 indicate fundamental misunderstanding; BSI escalates severity in subsequent rounds

checks.action: Verify PMCF plan includes: study objectives, patient population, endpoints, sample size justification, follow-up schedule, AND statistical analysis plan
checks.rationale: PM-001 appears across NCR and Clinical Review; insufficient PMCF justification is a recurring BSI finding

checks.action: For devices requiring sterilization: verify microbial control evidence and cleanroom certification are included and current
checks.rationale: MF-001 introduced by 鑫君特 Microbiologist Review; BSI modular system includes dedicated microbiologist panel

writer_guard_injection.injection_point: Before Writer Agent generates any CER section for BSI submission
writer_guard_injection.prompt_prefix:
You are authoring a Clinical Evaluation Report for submission to BSI (British Standards Institution). BSI employs a MODULAR REVIEW system with parallel expert panels (Main Job, Clinical, Technical, Microbiologist). Each panel evaluates different dimensions independently.

CRITICAL BSI-SPECIFIC RULES:
1. GSPR checklist MUST have every item addressed — non-applicable items require detailed justification, not just 'N/A'
2. Device description in §2 MUST include: name, all models/variants, EMDN code, MDR Class+Rule, intended purpose WITH patient population definition, contraindications, AND description of any novel features
3. If claiming equivalence: MDCG 2020-5 three-pillar structure (clinical/technical/biological) is MANDATORY; each pillar needs standalone evidence summary
4. For active devices: software lifecycle documentation list must be complete; every test report must trace to a design record
5. PMCF plan must be substantive — include objectives, population, endpoints, sample size justification, follow-up schedule, statistical plan
6. For implantable/invasive devices: include microbial control evidence and cleanroom certification
7. If this is R2+ resubmission: explicitly address HOW the root cause was fixed systemically, not just WHAT documents were updated

BSI REVIEW STYLE: BSI issues almost exclusively MAJOR findings (98%). They do not waste time on minor formatting issues. Every finding is substantive. Write as if every sentence will be scrutinized by a domain expert.

data_basis.data_quality_note: TÜV SÜD sample currently dominated by 青岛德迈迪 (14/14 records). One additional project (普美康, 2 records from batch1) contributes to BSI profile due to BSI assignment. Need 1-2 more TÜV SÜD projects to validate generalizability.

review_model.description: TÜV SÜD operates a sequential Deficiency Report (DR) model. Each round produces a formal DR document with standardized template. Multi-round reviews persistently pursue the same defect until fully resolved. Template standardization is extremely high.

dimensions.focus: Formal structured deficiency findings with mandatory response
dimensions.characteristics: Highly standardized template; 'Dear Sir or Madame' + 'Documents assessed' table + numbered DEF items

dimensions.focus: Manufacturer response to previous DR findings
dimensions.characteristics: Tracks closure status of previous findings; may reveal persistent unresolved issues

by_type.label: 器械描述缺陷
by_type.trend: 压倒性首位，三轮回补均围绕此缺陷

by_cer_section.note: §1 Device Description — TÜV SÜD的绝对核心关注

severity_profile.interpretation: TÜV SÜD issues predominantly Major findings (92.9%). The single Minor finding suggests a consistent high-threshold model, similar to BSI but with slightly more tolerance for minor observations in later rounds.

source_type_distribution.characteristics: Formal Deficiency Report with standardized template; single dominant format
Response document tracking closure of previous DR findings

round_behavior.description: TÜV SÜD exhibits a distinctive sequential persistence pattern: the same defect type (DO-001) is pursued across R1→R3 with increasing specificity.

round_distribution.focus: Initial identification of device description gaps
Deepening inquiry — 'reason not clearly defined / explained in detail'
Escalation to clinical evidence when description fixes insufficient

persistent_defect_pattern.specific_issue: 两种夹子形状 TIGA-LOK 和 LIGA-LOK 的设计原因/区别未阐明
persistent_defect_pattern.progression: R1: 'reason not clearly defined' → R2: 'not explained in detail' → R3: 'incomplete' + escalation to clinical evidence
persistent_defect_pattern.root_cause: 企业对产品变型的设计rationale缺乏法规视角的系统性阐述

round_behavior.interpretation: TÜV SÜD's sequential DR model means they will NOT move on to the next topic until the current one is fully resolved. A defect that persists to R3 is a signal of either (a) manufacturer's inability to articulate the design rationale, or (b) actual design history documentation gaps.

regulatory_reference_preference.description: MDCG 2020-5 (Clinical evaluation — equivalence) — most cited by far
MDR Annex XIV (Clinical evaluation and post-market clinical follow-up)

language_patterns: German-English bilingual header: 'TÜV SÜD Product Service GmbH'
Mandatory 'Documents assessed' table at document start
Numbered DEF items: DEF 1, DEF 2, DEF 3...
Each DEF follows format: finding + reference + requested action
Reference documents listed in structured table
extraction_risks: CRITICAL: 'Documents assessed' table occupies first 8000+ characters — must skip to first 'DEF 1' to find actual findings
Template text (salutation, company info, legal disclaimers) dominates document volume
Multiple file formats per round (docx, pdf, xlsx) with identical content — deduplication needed
Situation update files ('situ xlsx') may contain revised findings — must process latest version
extraction_fixes_applied: TÜV SÜD detection: if text contains 'TÜV SÜD Product Service GmbH' AND 'Documents assessed', locate first 'DEF 1' and truncate preceding text
Skip patterns expanded: TÜV SÜD-specific template lines (salutation, document list, legal text)

device_category_sensitivity.note: 产品结构看似简单，但设计选择的临床依据是TÜV SÜD的核心关切
普美康2条记录实际由BSI审评，TÜV SÜD样本未覆盖AED领域

deerflow_pre_check_rules.description: When submitting to TÜV SÜD, apply these pre-submission checks BEFORE Writer Agent output

checks.action: For EVERY product variant, model, or shape difference: explicitly document (a) the clinical/engineering reason for the difference, (b) how it impacts safety/performance/usability, (c) which variant is used in which clinical scenario
checks.rationale: DO-001 dominates at 64.3%; TÜV SÜD will persist across R1→R3 until design rationale is fully articulated

checks.action: Verify ALL available clinical data is considered/collected/provided; gap analysis must explain why any omitted data is not relevant
checks.rationale: EV-001 at 28.6% and increases with each round; TÜV SÜD escalates to clinical evidence when description is insufficient

checks.trigger: Submissions relying on literature-based clinical evidence
checks.action: Verify SOTA search covers all relevant databases with documented search strategy, inclusion/exclusion criteria, AND PRISMA-style flow diagram
checks.rationale: 青岛德迈迪 DR3 explicitly challenged SOTA search adequacy

checks.action: Verify CEP objectives, parameters, and endpoints are fully addressed in CER; any deviation requires explicit justification
checks.rationale: TÜV SÜD checks CEP→CER alignment rigorously; DR3 found clinical evaluation plan deficiencies

checks.action: If same defect appeared in previous DR, require complete redesign rationale (not incremental update). TÜV SÜD will NOT accept partial fixes.
checks.rationale: TÜV SÜD sequential model: they do NOT move to next topic until current is FULLY resolved

checks.action: Verify ALL safety and performance parameters in CEP have corresponding clinical data with adequate patient numbers
checks.rationale: 青岛德迈迪 DR3: 'Insufficient patient number in Safety/Performance parameter (e.g. rebleeding)'

writer_guard_injection.injection_point: Before Writer Agent generates any CER section for TÜV SÜD submission
writer_guard_injection.prompt_prefix:
You are authoring a Clinical Evaluation Report for submission to TÜV SÜD. TÜV SÜD uses a SEQUENTIAL DEFICIENCY REPORT model with extremely high template standardization. They will NOT proceed to the next review topic until the current finding is FULLY resolved.

CRITICAL TÜV SÜD-SPECIFIC RULES:
1. EVERY product variant, model, or shape difference MUST have explicit design rationale: WHY does this variant exist? HOW does it differ clinically? WHICH scenario uses WHICH variant?
2. Device description must be exhaustive — TÜV SÜD will question any aspect not explicitly documented; 'obvious' or 'implied' is not acceptable
3. ALL available clinical data must be collected and provided; gap analysis must justify any omissions with explicit reasoning
4. SOTA literature search must follow documented strategy with inclusion/exclusion criteria and PRISMA-style flow
5. Clinical Evaluation Plan (CEP) objectives MUST be fully addressed in CER; any deviation requires explicit justification
6. Safety and performance parameters must have adequate patient numbers in clinical data
7. If this is R2+ resubmission: do NOT provide incremental fixes. TÜV SÜD requires COMPLETE resolution. Restructure the entire section if needed.

TÜV SÜD REVIEW STYLE: Sequential, persistent, template-driven. They will ask the same question three times with increasing specificity. Write as if every design decision will be questioned.

template_fingerprint.description: TÜV SÜD DR template has a distinct fingerprint useful for automated extraction

review_model.description: DEKRA reviewers emphasize literature search methodology and SOTA justification. Strong focus on search protocol reproducibility and database selection rationale.

writer_guard_injection.injection_point: Before Writer Agent generates SOTA and literature search sections for DEKRA submission
writer_guard_injection.prompt_prefix:
You are authoring a Clinical Evaluation Report for submission to DEKRA. DEKRA reviewers place exceptional emphasis on literature search methodology and SOTA justification.

CRITICAL DEKRA-SPECIFIC RULES:
1. Every database selection must have explicit rationale (why PubMed + Embase + CT.gov, not just what databases).
2. Search protocol must be fully reproducible: include exact query strings, date stamps, filters, and hit counts per database.
3. SOTA benchmarks must clearly show the deduction chain: disease background -> endpoint selection -> benchmark value derivation -> clinical meaning.
4. Literature exclusion reasons must be individually documented, not summarized in aggregate.

deerflow_pre_check_rules: Verify search protocol reproducibility before Writer invocation
Check SOTA deduction chain completeness (all 7 steps)
Validate database selection rationale per device class

IMQ.nb_body_full_name: IMQ (Istituto Italiano del Marchio di Qualita)

review_model.description: IMQ reviewers focus on clinical evaluation methodology and PMCF planning. They expect clear demonstration of clinical benefit and structured post-market follow-up.

writer_guard_injection.injection_point: Before Writer Agent generates clinical evaluation and PMCF sections for IMQ submission
writer_guard_injection.prompt_prefix:
You are authoring a Clinical Evaluation Report for submission to IMQ. IMQ reviewers emphasize clinical evaluation methodology and PMCF planning completeness.

CRITICAL IMQ-SPECIFIC RULES:
1. Clinical benefit must be demonstrated with quantitative endpoints, not narrative descriptions.
2. PMCF plan must specify objectives, endpoints, sample size justification, and timeline — generic PMCF statements are rejected.
3. Every clinical claim must be traceable to a specific evidence record with appraisal result.
4. Benefit-risk conclusions must reference both clinical data and PMCF data separately.

deerflow_pre_check_rules: Verify PMCF plan has specific objectives and timelines
Check all clinical claims have evidence record traceability
Validate benefit-risk analysis references PMCF data

review_model.description: MEDCERT reviewers are known for rigorous equivalence demonstration requirements and benefit-risk quantification. They expect numerical comparison where possible.

writer_guard_injection.injection_point: Before Writer Agent generates equivalence and benefit-risk sections for MEDCERT submission
writer_guard_injection.prompt_prefix:
You are authoring a Clinical Evaluation Report for submission to MEDCERT. MEDCERT reviewers demand rigorous equivalence demonstration and quantitative benefit-risk analysis.

CRITICAL MEDCERT-SPECIFIC RULES:
1. Equivalence must be demonstrated across ALL THREE dimensions (technical, biological, clinical) with measurable comparisons.
2. Any difference between subject and equivalent device must have a documented impact analysis (difference -> mechanism -> evidence -> clinical impact).
3. Benefit-risk analysis must include quantitative comparison where data permits — narrative BR is insufficient.
4. Equivalence cannot be claimed if any dimension has missing data — mark as 'not demonstrated' instead.

deerflow_pre_check_rules: Check difference impact analysis for each dimension
Validate quantitative benefit-risk comparison where data exists

source: AUDIT_ARCHIVE R7 enriched profiles + 64 real NB records
